---
title: "Session 2: Reproducibility"
subtitle: "Foundations for reproducible research (with Lars Vilhuber)"
date: 2026-04-13
---

## Where this session fits

Guest-led by [Lars Vilhuber](https://www.ilr.cornell.edu/people/lars-vilhuber), AEA Data Editor. This first reproducibility session opens the block and sets up vocabulary and expectations.

Topics expected to be covered:

- Why reproducibility is a research-integrity issue, not a box-check.
- What top journals now require (AEA, REStud, JPE).
- openICPSR and the replication-package workflow.

## Materials

The session uses Lars's *Tutorial on Reproducibility, 2026*. Start at the landing page below; it is the orchestrator for the multi-day tutorial and the right entry point for the conceptual material in this session.

- **Primary**: [Tutorial on Reproducibility, 2026 (landing page)](https://larsvilhuber.github.io/tutorial-reproducibility-2026/)
- **Bonus**: [Self-checking reproducibility](https://larsvilhuber.github.io/self-checking-reproducibility/), a short companion on auditing your own replication package before submission.

Slides and additional references will be linked here after the session.
